description:
Penetration-testing consultants frequently need to keep tamper-proof backups of their raw
 scan results before they begin the more destructive stages of an engagement.  
 Your goal is to create a **single, versioned, compressed backup** of the three existing
 raw scan-result files that the consultant left under  

    /home/user/vuln_scans/raw/  

 and to generate the accompanying integrity and audit information.

 IMPORTANT:  The automated grader will look **strictly** at paths, file-names and the
 exact textual format of the files you create, so read the requirements below with care.

 1. Create the directory /home/user/vuln_scans/backup if it does not already exist.

 2. Pack the three files that already exist in /home/user/vuln_scans/raw/
      • initial_network.nmap  
      • ssh_bruteforce.hydra  
      • webdir_enum.gobuster  
    into **one** compressed tar archive named strictly  
       scans_backup_20230915.tar.gz  
    The archive must live directly under /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/  
    and, when listed with «tar -tzf», must show the three files with no leading path
    components – i.e. the entries inside the archive must appear exactly as  
       initial_network.nmap  
       ssh_bruteforce.hydra  
       webdir_enum.gobuster  

 3. Produce a SHA-256 manifest called  
       /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/scans_backup_20230915.sha256  
    containing **exactly three lines**, one per original file, with this precise field
    layout (two spaces between hash and filename, and a trailing newline after each line):

       <64-hex-chars>␠␠initial_network.nmap\n  
       <64-hex-chars>␠␠ssh_bruteforce.hydra\n  
       <64-hex-chars>␠␠webdir_enum.gobuster\n  

    Example of the required shape (hash values will of course differ):

       d2c7…3e  initial_network.nmap
       1b45…9a  ssh_bruteforce.hydra
       8ff0…11  webdir_enum.gobuster

 4. Create /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/backup.log and append **two** lines documenting
    what you just did.  The log lines must be in UTC and follow this exact pattern
    (square brackets, a single space after the timestamp, message text):

       [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] Archived 3 files into scans_backup_20230915.tar.gz (SIZE)\n  
       [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] SHA256 manifest saved to scans_backup_20230915.sha256\n  

    • Use the same timestamp (to the second) for both lines.  
    • Replace SIZE with the human-readable size reported by «du -h» (e.g. “12K”).  
    • Make sure the file ends with a trailing newline.

 5. Keep your solution to roughly five to ten shell commands; anything much longer is
    unnecessary.  Use standard coreutils available on a minimal Linux install only
    (tar, gzip, sha256sum, du, date, mkdir, echo, etc.).

 The grader will verify the following:

   a.  /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/scans_backup_20230915.tar.gz exists and contains the
       three files with the required internal paths.  

   b.  The manifest exists, has exactly three well-formed lines, and
       «sha256sum -c» passes when run inside /home/user/vuln_scans/backup.  

   c.  backup.log exists, has exactly the two required lines whose timestamps match
       each other and are in the correct format, and whose text matches
       the described template including the archive size.

 Do **not** rename, move or alter the original raw files; only back them up and record
 the required metadata.
truth:
Pre-existing environment before the agent starts:

 1. Directory hierarchy:
     /home/user/vuln_scans/
         └── raw/
              ├── initial_network.nmap
              ├── ssh_bruteforce.hydra
              └── webdir_enum.gobuster

 2. Exact contents of the raw files (each ends with a newline):

   /home/user/vuln_scans/raw/initial_network.nmap
   ------------------------------------------------
   # Nmap 7.93 scan initiated Fri Sep 15 13:45:00 2023 as: nmap -A -T4 192.168.1.0/24
   Host: 192.168.1.10 ()  Status: Up
   PORT     STATE SERVICE     VERSION
   22/tcp   open  ssh         OpenSSH 7.9p1 Ubuntu 10 (Ubuntu Linux; protocol 2.0)
   80/tcp   open  http        Apache httpd 2.4.29 ((Ubuntu))
   
   # Nmap done at Fri Sep 15 13:45:30 2023 -- 256 IP addresses (1 host up) scanned in 30.00 seconds
   \n

   /home/user/vuln_scans/raw/ssh_bruteforce.hydra
   ------------------------------------------------
   Hydra v9.1 (c) 2023 - Please do not use in unauthorised ways.
   Target: 192.168.1.10 - ssh
   [22][ssh] host: 192.168.1.10   login: root   password: toor
   1 of 1 target successfully completed, 1 valid password found
   \n

   /home/user/vuln_scans/raw/webdir_enum.gobuster
   ------------------------------------------------
   ===============================================================
   Gobuster v3.3.0
   ===============================================================
   /admin (Status: 301)
   /login (Status: 200)
   /config.php (Status: 403)
   ===============================================================
   \n

 3. Permissions:
    Everything under /home/user belongs to the unprivileged “user” account
    with at least rwx rights for that user, so the agent can freely create the
    /home/user/vuln_scans/backup directory and all required files.

 Verification details the grader will use (do not reveal in public description):

   • Archive must be: /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/scans_backup_20230915.tar.gz
     Its «tar -tzf» output must be exactly (order ignored):
       initial_network.nmap
       ssh_bruteforce.hydra
       webdir_enum.gobuster

   • Manifest must be: /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/scans_backup_20230915.sha256
     It must contain exactly three newline-terminated records.  Each record must
     have:  64 lowercase hexadecimal characters, two spaces, one of the three
     filenames as specified.

     The grader will recompute the hashes from the original files and compare.

   • Log file must be: /home/user/vuln_scans/backup/backup.log
     It must contain exactly two lines, both starting with:
        [\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}]␠
     Strings after the timestamp must match precisely the two messages in the
     public spec, including the filename, size substring «(SIZE)», and
     trailing newline on each line.  The grader allows any SIZE token acceptable
     by «du -h» (e.g. “8.0K”, “12K”, “16K”).

   • The timestamps of the two lines must be identical down to the second and
     must be valid UTC (checked via date ‑d).

 No other files, directories or text are required.
